channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump orders strikes on Iran-backed Houthi rebels in Yemen and issues new warning
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump said he ordered a series of airstrikes on the Houthi-held areas in Yemen on Saturday, promising to use “overwhelming lethal force” until the Iran-backed rebels cease their attacks on shipping along a vital maritime corridor.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, SAMY MAGDY and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Alien Enemies Act: What to know about a 1798 law that Trump has invoked for deportations
item.description: President Donald Trump has invoked the Alien Enemies Act for the first time since World War II, granting himself sweeping powers under a centuries-old law to deport people associated with a Venezuelan gang.
item.dc:creator: Tim Sullivan And Elliot Spagat, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump invokes wartime Alien Enemies Act of 1798 to target violent illegal immigrant street gangs
item.description: President Donald Trump invoked an 18th century law, used only during the War of 1812, World War I and World War II Saturday to curb illegal immigrant gangs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: VA denies policy change after internal memo rescinds transgender veterans’ guidance
item.description: In an internal VA memo seen by NPR, the VA says it's rescinding a directive that contains detailed guidance on the kind of care transgender veterans can receive at VA facilities.
item.dc:creator: Huo Jingnan, MPR News

item.title: Trump enacts a 1790s law to target ‘alien enemies’ for detention and deportation
item.description: In a presidential action Saturday, Trump invoked the wartime authority Alien Enemies Act, targeting members of the Venezuelan gang Tren de Aragua to immediate detention and deportation.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Walz hits road to rip Trump policies as he works to keep his own national profile up
item.description: Gov. Tim Walz, the 2024 Democratic vice presidential nominee, held forums in Iowa and Nebraska. He is using them to dissect Trump administration actions and perhaps boost his own standing in the soul-searching Democratic Party.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: President Trump signs continuing resolution, officially averting a shutdown
item.description: A government shutdown has officially been averted, as President Donald Trump signs the continuing resolution into law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fetterman claps back at Ocasio-Cortez on government funding vote
item.description: Sen. John Fetterman (D-Pa.), who announced days ago that he would vote for a House-passed funding bill to avoid a government shutdown, said he doesn’t care about firebrand Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez’s (D-N.Y.) harsh criticism of Democratic senators who will vote to advance the controversial bill.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Trump signs order to gut staff at Voice of America and other US-funded media organizations
item.description: President Donald Trump is ordering his administration to gut Voice of America and other pro-democracy media organizations run by the U.S. government.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Riccardi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Federal agencies plan for mass layoffs as Trump's workforce cuts continue
item.description: Thursday's deadline for federal agencies to submit plans for large-scale layoffs kicks off a new phase in the dramatic restructuring of how the government operates. Here's what that looks like.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler, MPR News

item.title: China's US influence could face crackdown under slate of new bills
item.description: U.S. Sen. James Lankford, R-Okla., rolled out several bills aimed at countering Chinese influence and ownership in the U.S. as tensions grow.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House Democrats to hold 'Day of Action' to push back against GOP-backed spending bill
item.description: House Democrats will hold a "Day of Action" across the country on Tuesday to push back against the GOP-backed spending bill to avert a government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats confront limits of their minority power after bruising shutdown vote
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Senate Democrats were grim Friday as they left Washington after a brutal 10-week stretch that consistently showed the limits of their power in the minority — and culminated with a deeply personal rupture over how to best counter President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democratic Party fractures in government shutdown fight, with anger running high
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Democratic Party was fracturing Friday as a torrent of frustration and anger was unleashed at Senate Democrats, led by Sen.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, MATT BROWN and STEVE PEOPLES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Injunction lifted on Trump executive orders slashing federal DEI support
item.description: An appeals court on Friday overturned a previous judge's decision to block President Donald Trump's executive orders surrounding diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) programs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says he was being a 'bit sarcastic' when he promised to end Russia-Ukraine war in 24 hours
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump said Friday that he was “being a little bit sarcastic” when he repeatedly claimed as a candidate that he would have the Russia-Ukraine war solved within 24 hours — and even before he even took office.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump undoes stack of 'harmful' Biden executive orders, shrinks multiple agencies
item.description: The White House on Friday said President Donald Trump took more actions to downsize the federal government through several executive orders.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Appeals court lifts blocks on Trump's orders restricting diversity, equity and inclusion programs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An appeals court on Friday lifted a block on executive orders seeking to end government support for diversity, equity and inclusion programs, handing the Trump administration a win after a string of setbacks defending President Donald Trump’s agenda from dozens of lawsuits.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate approves funding bill hours before shutdown deadline, sending to Trump for signature
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate passed a Republican-led spending bill Friday hours before a government shutdown, overcoming sharp Democratic opposition to the measure and sending it to President Donald Trump to be signed into law.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Oklahoma schools chief won’t abandon classroom Bible effort
item.description: Last year, Ryan Walters issued a mandate for schools to incorporate lessons on the Bible in grades 5 through 12.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Ron Nessen, press secretary to President Ford, dies at 90
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Ron Nessen, a veteran broadcast journalist who was press secretary for President Gerald Ford and sought to restore the integrity that the position had lost during the Nixon administration, has died. He was 90.
item.dc:creator: DOUGLASS K. DANIEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge rules against advocates trying to help migrants sent to Guantanamo and to stop more transfers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge ruled Friday against immigration and civil rights advocates attempting to help migrants who had been sent to the Guantanamo Bay military base — and trying to prevent further transfers — days after the Trump administration transferred all migrants out of the facility
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judges don't intervene after the Trump administration says it's stopped destroying USAID records
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Federal judges in two separate lawsuits refused Friday to order the Trump administration to not destroy U.S. Agency for International Development records after it said it has disposed of only old or unneeded documents and is no longer destroying records anyway.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST and JOHN HANNA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republican legislation seeks to ban Chinese nationals from studying in the US
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A group of House Republicans on Friday put forward legislation seeking to prevent Chinese students from studying in American schools, as some U.S. lawmakers are targeting China over national security concerns. Rep. Riley Moore, R-W.Va.
item.dc:creator: FU TING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man escorted out of NC Republican's town hall
item.description: Rep. Chuck Edwards (R-N.C.) faced a wave of backlash from constituents while hosting a Thursday town hall in Asheville, which he held against the advice of party leaders. One attendee, who identified himself as a veteran, was removed from the event. “He has nothing to say but lies.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: AOC, Sanders tell supporters to mask up for West Coast town halls
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders, I-Vt., and Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, D-N.Y., are taking the "Fighting Oligarchy" rallies out West next week – and advising supporters to mask up to participate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Spending bill to avert shutdown successfully beats filibuster with hours until deadline
item.description: The Senate voted on a key procedural motion regarding the House-passed stopgap spending bill on Friday. If a measure is not passed by midnight, the government will enter a shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump to increase steel and aluminum tariffs on Canada to 50 percent in response to electricity surcharge
item.description: The uncertainty around Trump's tariff plans have led to a downturn in the stock market, and the president has not ruled out the possibility of a recession this year.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Wall Street ends manic day modestly lower after briefly dropping more than 10% below its record
item.description: The U.S. stock market fell further Tuesday following President Donald Trump’s latest escalation in his trade war, briefly pulling Wall Street 10% below its record set last month. And like it's been most of the past few weeks, the market’s slide on Tuesday was erratic and dizzying.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
